div1.entryFree: Hail, subst. raindrops congealed in falling: Mids. I, 1, 244. All's V, 3, 33. Ant. III, 13, 159. Metaphorically used of any thing poured out fast and copiously, especially of language: not a heart could scape the h. of his all-hurting aim, Compl. 310. as thick as h. came post with post, Mcb. I, 3, 97 (O. Edd. tale). cf. Mids. I, 1, 244.
